content:
Case 18:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 34
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Follow-up for transfusion-dependent pancytopenia with persistent severe neutropenia and sequelae of disseminated fusariosis
- Vital signs: Temp 37.7 °C, BP 104/66 mmHg, HR 102/min (regular), RR 18/min, SpO₂ 94% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient hematology follow-up in a patient with longstanding PNH/aplastic marrow failure and recent disseminated fungal infection
Capabilities: You are seeing a profoundly cytopenic, heavily transfusion-dependent patient in the outpatient clinic. You can review symptoms, exam, current medications, cytopenia pattern, and recent cross-sectional imaging. You decide on urgency and scope of inpatient versus outpatient diagnostics and whether to proceed to repeat bone marrow evaluation and extended infectious work-up.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2024-09-15: The patient presented for follow-up with increasing transfusion requirements, requiring RBC transfusions weekly and platelet transfusions up to 3–4 times per week. ANC remained critically low at 0.1–0.2 × 10⁹/L. She reported skin lesions that showed gradual healing with postinflammatory hyperpigmentation and impaired vision with persistent blurriness and reduced acuity. She reported intermittent cough with occasional blood-streaked sputum and chronic nasal congestion.
Her general condition was poor with pronounced fatigue, weakness, and emotional distress. She appeared tearful and anxious during consultation, expressing significant fear of renewed long hospital stays, remembering the prolonged summer admission for disseminated fusariosis. ECOG performance status was 1.
Current medication: pegcetacoplan 1080 mg s.c. twice weekly, eltrombopag 150 mg 1-0-0, ciclosporin A 150 mg 1-0-1, voriconazole 200 mg 1-0-1, terbinafine 250 mg 1-0-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, cotrimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mo/Wed/Fri), sitagliptin 100 mg 1-0-0, pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1, sertraline 50 mg 1-0-0, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, filgrastim 30 Mio IU s.c. 2–3×/week as needed, epoetin alfa 40,000 IU s.c. once weekly, lorazepam 1 mg on demand for anxiety or insomnia.
Vital signs: temperature 37.7 °C, blood pressure 104/66 mmHg, heart rate 102/min (regular), respiratory rate 18/min, oxygen saturation 94% on room air.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential"}}

Cbc Chemistry:
CBC: Hb 7.8 g/dL, WBC 0.9 × 10⁹/L, ANC 0.15 × 10⁹/L, Plt 18 × 10⁹/L; MCV 95 fL, MCH 31 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 11%, lymphocytes 64%, monocytes 19%, eosinophils 4%, basophils 2%.
Reticulocytes: 12 × 10⁹/L.
Coagulation: aPTT 35 sec, INR 1.3.
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 4.6 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 28 mg/dL, AST 41 U/L, ALT 44 U/L, bilirubin 1.6 mg/dL, LDH 328 U/L, uric acid 6.2 mg/dL, CRP 10.8 mg/dL, haptoglobin 21 mg/dL, ferritin 4950 ng/mL.
Immunology: CD4⁺ 380/µL, CD8⁺ 590/µL (CD4/CD8 ratio 0.64). Immunoglobulins: IgG 840 mg/dL (normal), IgA 68 mg/dL, IgM 42 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results

content:
'bone_marrow' available in round 2.
Bone marrow biopsy/aspirate findings are part of the case but are not available in this round. Consider requesting available items such as CBC/chemistry, history and physical, or baseline imaging instead.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "chest CT scan"}}

Baseline Imaging:
2024-09-18 Abdominal ultrasound: Normal-sized liver (14.8 cm) with homogeneous echotexture, no focal lesions. Intra- and extrahepatic bile ducts not dilated (DHC 5 mm). Gallbladder poorly visualized (postprandial). Spleen 11.2 × 4.6 cm, regular margins, no focal lesions. Kidneys normal in size and morphology (left 10.8 cm, right 11.1 cm) with preserved corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis, no lymphadenopathy, no ascites.
2024-09-18 Echocardiogram: Sinus rhythm, preserved LV ejection fraction (55%), normal RV function, no relevant valvular disease or pericardial effusion.
2024-09-19 CT of the paranasal sinuses: Mucosal thickening in both maxillary sinuses (right > left) and ethmoidal cells, consistent with chronic fungal sinusitis. No fluid levels, bone erosion, or orbital involvement.
2024-09-20 CT thorax: Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (1–1.5 cm), partly cavitary with halo sign, consistent with residual pulmonary fungal lesions. No pleural effusion or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Refractory severe aplastic anemia (SAA) with PNH clone (PNH/aplastic anemia overlap syndrome), complicated by disseminated fusariosis with residual pulmonary and sinonasal disease in the setting of prolonged profound neutropenia", "differentials": ["Hypocellular MDS with PNH clone (clonal evolution from aplastic anemia)", "MDS with excess blasts evolving from prior IST-treated aplastic anemia", "Treatment-related marrow aplasia (cyclosporine, eltrombopag insufficiency)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate urgent allogeneic HSCT evaluation: confirm HLA typing (patient + family), activate unrelated volunteer donor search via registry (DKMS/NMDP), and refer to transplant center \u2014 this is the only curative option for refractory SAA/PNH", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Optimize antifungal regimen: add liposomal amphotericin B (3\u20135 mg/kg/day IV) to current voriconazole + terbinafine combination given Fusarium spp. azole resistance risk; confirm voriconazole trough levels (target 2\u20135 mg/L) and obtain Fusarium isolate susceptibility (MIC) if not yet done", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Admit for inpatient monitoring and supportive care intensification: continue leuko-depleted irradiated RBC transfusions (target Hb \u2265 8 g/dL), platelet transfusions to maintain > 20 \u00d7 10\u2079/L, filgrastim G-CSF daily during active infection; continue pegcetacoplan, valaciclovir, cotrimoxazole prophylaxis", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Perform repeat bone marrow biopsy with aspirate (cytomorphology, cytogenetics including FISH panel for MDS-associated changes, flow cytometry for PNH clone size and blasts, CD34 quantification) to exclude clonal evolution and re-assess cellularity before transplant conditioning", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Check serum beta-D-glucan, Fusarium galactomannan, and serial CT thorax at 4\u20136 weeks to document treatment response and determine whether fusariosis is adequately controlled prior to HSCT conditioning", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Nephrology review for cyclosporine-associated nephrotoxicity (creatinine 1.4 mg/dL): consider cyclosporine trough levels and dose adjustment; note renal function relevant to conditioning agent dosing and amphotericin B safety", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Eltrombopag continuation at 150 mg daily pending HSCT: reassess hematological response after 4 weeks; if no meaningful improvement in platelet or ANC recovery, document treatment failure to support transplant urgency", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Multidisciplinary psycho-oncology and social work referral: address significant anxiety, PTSD-like fear of re-hospitalization, and depression (on sertraline); optimize lorazepam use and consider structured psychological support program", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Iron chelation planning: ferritin 4,950 ng/mL reflects transfusional iron overload; defer active chelation until post-HSCT engraftment but document cumulative transfusion burden for pre-transplant workup", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Refractory severe aplastic anemia (SAA) with PNH clone (PNH/aplastic anemia overlap syndrome), complicated by disseminated fusariosis with residual pulmonary and sinonasal disease in the setting of prolonged profound neutropenia
differentials: Hypocellular MDS with PNH clone (clonal evolution from aplastic anemia)
MDS with excess blasts evolving from prior IST-treated aplastic anemia

treatment_plan.action: Initiate urgent allogeneic HSCT evaluation: confirm HLA typing (patient + family), activate unrelated volunteer donor search via registry (DKMS/NMDP), and refer to transplant center — this is the only curative option for refractory SAA/PNH

Optimize antifungal regimen: add liposomal amphotericin B (3–5 mg/kg/day IV) to current voriconazole + terbinafine combination given Fusarium spp. azole resistance risk; confirm voriconazole trough levels (target 2–5 mg/L) and obtain Fusarium isolate susceptibility (MIC) if not yet done

Admit for inpatient monitoring and supportive care intensification: continue leuko-depleted irradiated RBC transfusions (target Hb ≥ 8 g/dL), platelet transfusions to maintain > 20 × 10⁹/L, filgrastim G-CSF daily during active infection; continue pegcetacoplan, valaciclovir, cotrimoxazole prophylaxis

Perform repeat bone marrow biopsy with aspirate (cytomorphology, cytogenetics including FISH panel for MDS-associated changes, flow cytometry for PNH clone size and blasts, CD34 quantification) to exclude clonal evolution and re-assess cellularity before transplant conditioning

Check serum beta-D-glucan, Fusarium galactomannan, and serial CT thorax at 4–6 weeks to document treatment response and determine whether fusariosis is adequately controlled prior to HSCT conditioning

Nephrology review for cyclosporine-associated nephrotoxicity (creatinine 1.4 mg/dL): consider cyclosporine trough levels and dose adjustment; note renal function relevant to conditioning agent dosing and amphotericin B safety

Eltrombopag continuation at 150 mg daily pending HSCT: reassess hematological response after 4 weeks; if no meaningful improvement in platelet or ANC recovery, document treatment failure to support transplant urgency

Multidisciplinary psycho-oncology and social work referral: address significant anxiety, PTSD-like fear of re-hospitalization, and depression (on sertraline); optimize lorazepam use and consider structured psychological support program

Iron chelation planning: ferritin 4,950 ng/mL reflects transfusional iron overload; defer active chelation until post-HSCT engraftment but document cumulative transfusion burden for pre-transplant workup

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "correct", "differentials": ["correct", "correct", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Post–bone marrow work-up visit with new cytogenetic data
Capabilities: You now have bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, histology, and karyotype results. You can integrate hypocellular marrow findings, monosomy 7, and the known PNH clone, and reassess the diagnosis and risk profile. You decide whether standard immunosuppression remains appropriate or whether clonal evolution mandates a change in strategy and transplant planning.
Request additional information or solve.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results including cytogenetics"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results including cytogenetics

content:
Bone Marrow:
2024-09-22: Bone marrow aspiration showed marked reduction of granulopoiesis and virtually absent megakaryopoiesis. Erythropoiesis was markedly reduced with dysplastic features including nuclear irregularities, megaloblastoid changes, and occasional binucleated forms. Myelopoiesis was strongly suppressed with left shift, hypogranular neutrophils, and pseudo–Pelger-like forms. Plasma cells were slightly increased but morphologically unremarkable. No blast excess (<5%).
Flow cytometry demonstrated a very low cellularity sample with predominance of T-lymphocytes and an inverted CD4/CD8 ratio (0.6). A minimal residual population of myeloid precursors was present without aberrant antigen expression. No evidence of clonal B-lymphocytes, plasma cell dyscrasia, or blast population was detected.
Histology (biopsy) revealed hypocellular marrow spaces (cellularity <10%) largely replaced by adipose tissue. Sparse hematopoietic islands showed residual erythroid precursors with dysplasia, isolated myeloid elements, and almost no megakaryocytes. Small foci of reactive lymphoid infiltrates were present. Iron stain showed increased iron stores and occasional ring sideroblasts (<5%). Reticulin stain showed no marrow fibrosis (MF-0).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetics and karyotype results (including FISH panel)"}}

response.request: cytogenetics and karyotype results (including FISH panel)

Cytogenetics:
Preliminary cytogenetics: 46,XX[2] / 45,XX,-7[18].

Complicated by active/residual disseminated fusariosis.", "differentials": ["Refractory severe aplastic anemia with coincidental clonal cytogenetic abnormality (less likely: 18/20 metaphases with -7 indicates dominant clone)", "Therapy-related MDS (t-MDS) secondary to prior immunosuppressive therapy (cyclosporine, eltrombopag)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Reclassify diagnosis as high-risk MDS (monosomy 7, IPSS-R High) and expedite allogeneic HSCT: accelerate HLA-matched sibling or unrelated donor search, target transplant as soon as fusariosis is sufficiently controlled \u2014 this is the only curative option and delay worsens outcome", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discontinue cyclosporine A: IST is contraindicated in clonally evolved MDS with monosomy 7 as it provides no benefit and may promote further clonal selection; taper gradually to avoid rebound", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Optimize and intensify antifungal coverage for fusariosis as prerequisite for transplant conditioning: confirm voriconazole trough levels (target 2\u20135 mg/L), add or substitute liposomal amphotericin B (3\u20135 mg/kg/day IV) given Fusarium resistance profile, and obtain updated susceptibility data; fusariosis must be radiologically stable before conditioning", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider hypomethylating agent (azacitidine 75 mg/m\u00b2 SC days 1\u20137 per 28-day cycle) as bridge-to-transplant therapy if HSCT cannot proceed within 4\u20136 weeks, to suppress the monosomy 7 clone and reduce transformation risk while maintaining transfusion bridging", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Perform serial CT thorax and paranasal sinus CT (4\u20136 weeks) with beta-D-glucan and Fusarium-specific galactomannan to document infectious response and determine transplant eligibility window", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue intensive transfusion support (irradiated, leukodepleted RBCs targeting Hb \u2265 8 g/dL; platelets targeting > 20 \u00d7 10\u2079/L), G-CSF filgrastim, pegcetacoplan, valaciclovir, and cotrimoxazole prophylaxis throughout transplant preparation", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Refer to transplant center for pre-HSCT workup including formal HCT-CI scoring, dental clearance, ophthalmology evaluation (impaired vision from prior fusariosis), and infectious disease co-management for antifungal strategy peri-conditioning", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue psycho-oncology support and optimize anxiolytic/antidepressant regimen (sertraline, lorazepam prn); prepare patient psychologically for transplant admission using structured counseling and advance care planning discussions", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Hypocellular MDS with monosomy 7 (clonal evolution from prior aplastic anemia/PNH overlap syndrome), WHO 2022 category: MDS with defining cytogenetic abnormality — monosomy 7; IPSS-R High-risk. Complicated by active/residual disseminated fusariosis.
differentials: Refractory severe aplastic anemia with coincidental clonal cytogenetic abnormality (less likely: 18/20 metaphases with -7 indicates dominant clone)
Therapy-related MDS (t-MDS) secondary to prior immunosuppressive therapy (cyclosporine, eltrombopag)

treatment_plan.action: Reclassify diagnosis as high-risk MDS (monosomy 7, IPSS-R High) and expedite allogeneic HSCT: accelerate HLA-matched sibling or unrelated donor search, target transplant as soon as fusariosis is sufficiently controlled — this is the only curative option and delay worsens outcome
Discontinue cyclosporine A: IST is contraindicated in clonally evolved MDS with monosomy 7 as it provides no benefit and may promote further clonal selection; taper gradually to avoid rebound

Optimize and intensify antifungal coverage for fusariosis as prerequisite for transplant conditioning: confirm voriconazole trough levels (target 2–5 mg/L), add or substitute liposomal amphotericin B (3–5 mg/kg/day IV) given Fusarium resistance profile, and obtain updated susceptibility data; fusariosis must be radiologically stable before conditioning

Consider hypomethylating agent (azacitidine 75 mg/m² SC days 1–7 per 28-day cycle) as bridge-to-transplant therapy if HSCT cannot proceed within 4–6 weeks, to suppress the monosomy 7 clone and reduce transformation risk while maintaining transfusion bridging
Perform serial CT thorax and paranasal sinus CT (4–6 weeks) with beta-D-glucan and Fusarium-specific galactomannan to document infectious response and determine transplant eligibility window

Continue intensive transfusion support (irradiated, leukodepleted RBCs targeting Hb ≥ 8 g/dL; platelets targeting > 20 × 10⁹/L), G-CSF filgrastim, pegcetacoplan, valaciclovir, and cotrimoxazole prophylaxis throughout transplant preparation

Refer to transplant center for pre-HSCT workup including formal HCT-CI scoring, dental clearance, ophthalmology evaluation (impaired vision from prior fusariosis), and infectious disease co-management for antifungal strategy peri-conditioning

Continue psycho-oncology support and optimize anxiolytic/antidepressant regimen (sertraline, lorazepam prn); prepare patient psychologically for transplant admission using structured counseling and advance care planning discussions

Round 3 begins.
Setting: High-risk marrow failure and MDS transplant-planning discussion in the context of invasive fungal disease
Capabilities: You have full information including NGS (TET2, DNMT3A), detailed PNH clone quantification, prior IST response, and the course of disseminated fusariosis with ocular and pulmonary involvement. You must define the final diagnosis, antifungal strategy, and an allogeneic SCT plan, balancing curative intent against infectious risk and the patient's reluctance toward prolonged hospitalization.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

continuation risks promoting further clonal dominance and does not address MDS pathobiology", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Intensify antifungal regimen by adding liposomal amphotericin B (5\u201310 mg/kg/day IV) to existing voriconazole for dual-agent Fusarium coverage; confirm voriconazole trough levels (target 2\u20135 mg/L); obtain formal Fusarium isolate susceptibility (MIC voriconazole, amphotericin B); plan antifungal continuation through HSCT conditioning and engraftment", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Urgent ophthalmology consultation for ocular fusariosis with reduced visual acuity: evaluate for fungal endophthalmitis/chorioretinitis; consider intravitreal voriconazole or amphotericin B injection and vitreoretinal surgery referral if endophthalmitis confirmed", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discontinue eltrombopag: thrombopoietin receptor agonist has no evidence of benefit in MDS with monosomy 7 and TET2/DNMT3A mutations; there is a theoretical risk of promoting clonal expansion of the monosomy 7 clone; discontinue as part of transition from aplastic anemia to MDS-directed management", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue pegcetacoplan for the PNH clone throughout the pre-transplant period to prevent complement-mediated hemolysis and thrombotic complications; plan elective discontinuation after confirmed HSCT engraftment and PNH clone clearance", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue irradiated leukodepleted RBC transfusions (target Hb \u2265 8 g/dL) and platelet transfusions (target > 20 \u00d7 10\u2079/L), and continue filgrastim G-CSF 30 MIU SC 3\u20135\u00d7/week to support neutrophil counts during active fusariosis and throughout transplant preparation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Serial CT thorax and paranasal sinuses every 4\u20136 weeks with serum beta-D-glucan and Fusarium biomarkers to document radiological and serological response to antifungal therapy and define the transplant eligibility window", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Hypocellular MDS with defining cytogenetic abnormality (monosomy 7 in 18/20 metaphases) and somatic TET2/DNMT3A mutations, representing clonal evolution from aplastic anemia/PNH overlap syndrome; IPSS-R Very High risk (score ~7: cytogenetics Poor=3, Hb<8=1.5, Plt<50=1, ANC<0.8=1.5). Complicated by active disseminated fusariosis (pulmonary, sinonasal, ocular, cutaneous) during prolonged severe neutropenia (ANC 0.15 × 10⁹/L).
differentials: Aplastic anemia with clonal somatic mutations (CHIP/CCUS) without MDS — unlikely given dominant monosomy 7 clone in 90% of metaphases, multilineage dysplasia, and ring sideroblasts
Secondary (therapy-related) MDS following prolonged immunosuppressive therapy (cyclosporine, eltrombopag) for aplastic anemia/PNH

treatment_plan.action: Expedite allogeneic HSCT with reduced-intensity conditioning (fludarabine/busulfan or fludarabine/melphalan RIC): activate urgent HLA-matched sibling or MUD/unrelated donor search; target transplant window once fusariosis demonstrates radiological stability (CT nodule stability over 4–6 weeks); plan antifungal continuation through conditioning and engraftment; RIC preferred over MAC given active infection burden and compromised performance

Taper and discontinue cyclosporine A over 4–6 weeks: IST has no role in MDS with monosomy 7 and somatic mutations (TET2/DNMT3A); continuation risks promoting further clonal dominance and does not address MDS pathobiology

Intensify antifungal regimen by adding liposomal amphotericin B (5–10 mg/kg/day IV) to existing voriconazole for dual-agent Fusarium coverage; confirm voriconazole trough levels (target 2–5 mg/L); obtain formal Fusarium isolate susceptibility (MIC voriconazole, amphotericin B); plan antifungal continuation through HSCT conditioning and engraftment

Urgent ophthalmology consultation for ocular fusariosis with reduced visual acuity: evaluate for fungal endophthalmitis/chorioretinitis; consider intravitreal voriconazole or amphotericin B injection and vitreoretinal surgery referral if endophthalmitis confirmed

Discontinue eltrombopag: thrombopoietin receptor agonist has no evidence of benefit in MDS with monosomy 7 and TET2/DNMT3A mutations; there is a theoretical risk of promoting clonal expansion of the monosomy 7 clone; discontinue as part of transition from aplastic anemia to MDS-directed management

Continue pegcetacoplan for the PNH clone throughout the pre-transplant period to prevent complement-mediated hemolysis and thrombotic complications; plan elective discontinuation after confirmed HSCT engraftment and PNH clone clearance

Continue irradiated leukodepleted RBC transfusions (target Hb ≥ 8 g/dL) and platelet transfusions (target > 20 × 10⁹/L), and continue filgrastim G-CSF 30 MIU SC 3–5×/week to support neutrophil counts during active fusariosis and throughout transplant preparation

Serial CT thorax and paranasal sinuses every 4–6 weeks with serum beta-D-glucan and Fusarium biomarkers to document radiological and serological response to antifungal therapy and define the transplant eligibility window
